**Proof of Deploy 1 Pager** 

**Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud**

■  **Hackathon Overview**

| Theme | Build AI Agents using Qwen Cloud models & APIs |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Tracks** | 5 — MemoryAgent • AI Showrunner • Agent Society • Autopilot Agent • EdgeAgent |
| **Team Size** | Individuals, Teams, or Organizations |
| **Development** | May 26 – July 9, 2026 |
| **Final Submission** | July 9, 2026 \- 2 PM PT |
| **Judging Period** | July 10 – July 31, 2026 |
| **Results** | August 7, 2026 |
| **IP Ownership** | Participants retain full ownership |
|  |  |

■  **The Five Tracks**

•       **MemoryAgent:** Build agents with persistent, structured memory that learn and recall context across sessions.

•       **AI Showrunner:** Create AI-driven content production pipelines — video, audio, scripts, or multimedia.

•       **Agent Society:** Design multi-agent systems that collaborate, negotiate, or compete in shared environments.

•       **Autopilot Agent:** Develop autonomous agents that handle real-world workflows end-to-end with minimal human input.

•       **EdgeAgent:** Optimize AI agents for edge deployment — low latency, resource-constrained, on-device inference.

■  **Judging Criteria (Weighted)**

| Criterion | Weight | What Judges Look For |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Innovation & AI Creativity** | **30%** | Is the architecture high-quality, with strong modularity, scalability, and error handling? Does the project reflect clean code and non-trivial logic? Does the tech stack show sophistication via advanced patterns and thoughtful adoption?  |
| **Technical Depth & Engineering** | **30%** | Does the project make sophisticated use of QwenCloud APIs (e.g., custom skills, MCP integrations)? Does the project demonstrate algorithmic or engineering innovation through novel solutions, custom components, or performance optimization? |
| **Problem Value & Impact** | **25%** | Does the project have real-world relevance by solving an authentic technical or business pain point? Does the project have scalability potential for productization or open-source community adoption?  |
| **Presentation & Documentation** | **15%** | Is the technical demo clear, with the key logic visualized effectively? Is the documentation clear, including architecture docs that describe the project? |

■  **Submission Checklist**

* Provide a URL to your code repository for judging and testing. The repository must contain all necessary source code, assets, and instructions required for the project to be functional. The repository must be public and open source by including an open source license file. This license should be detectable and visible at the top of the repository page (in the About section).  
* 1-3-minute video demo (max) showing the agent in action  
* Architecture Diagram showing a clear visual representation of your system  
*  Written summary explaining the features and functionality  
* Proof of Alibaba Cloud Deployment using one of the below sample screenshots demonstrating that the backend is running on Alibaba Cloud.  
*  Open-source frameworks OK; direct repo cloning is not allowed

■  **Prize Structure ($70,000+ Total)**

| Award | Count | Prize per Winner |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Track Winners** | 5 | $10,000 each ($7K cash \+ $3K credits) |
| **Best Blog Authors** | 10 | $1,000 each ($500 cash \+ $500 credits) |
| **Honorable Mentions** | 10 | $1,000 each ($500 cash \+ $500 credits) |

 

**QUICKSTART — Base Code for Participants**

Copy-paste these snippets to get started with Qwen Cloud APIs in minutes.

■  **1\. Get Your API Key**

Sign up at qwencloud.com, then generate a key at home.qwencloud.com/api-keys.

Set it as an environment variable (never hardcode secrets):

\# Linux / macOS  
export DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY="sk-your-key-here"  
   
\# Windows PowerShell  
$env:DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY \= "sk-your-key-here"  
 

■  **2\. Python — Chat Completion (OpenAI SDK Compatible)**

Qwen Cloud is fully compatible with the OpenAI SDK. Install with: pip install openai

import os  
from openai import OpenAI  
   
client \= OpenAI(  
    api\_key=os.getenv("DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY"),  
    base\_url="https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1"  
)  
   
completion \= client.chat.completions.create(  
	model="qwen3.7-plus",  
	messages=\[  
    	{"role": "user", "content": "Summarize the benefits of solar energy."}  
	\]  
)  
   
print(completion.choices\[0\].message.content)  
 

■  **3\. Python — Streaming Responses**

stream \= client.chat.completions.create(  
	model="qwen3.7-max",  
	messages=\[  
    	{"role": "user", "content": "Write a haiku about AI agents."}  
	\],  
	stream=True  
)  
   
for chunk in stream:  
	if chunk.choices\[0\].delta.content:  
    	print(chunk.choices\[0\].delta.content, end="")  
 

■  **4\. Python — Function Calling (Tool Use)**

tools \= \[{  
	"type": "function",  
	"function": {  
    	"name": "get\_weather",  
    	"description": "Get weather for a city",  
    	"parameters": {  
        	"type": "object",  
        	"properties": {  
            	"city": {"type": "string", "description": "City name"}  
        	},  
        	"required": \["city"\]  
    	}  
	}  
}\]  
   
response \= client.chat.completions.create(  
	model="qwen3.7-plus",  
	messages=\[{"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in Jakarta?"}\],  
	tools=tools  
)  
   
print(response.choices\[0\].message.tool\_calls)  
 

■  **5\. Python — Image Generation (Wan2.6)**

response \= client.images.generate(  
	model="wan2.6-t2i",  
	prompt="A futuristic city skyline at sunset with flying cars",  
	size="1024x1024",  
	n=1  
)  
   
print(response.data\[0\].url)  
 

■  **6\. Python — Text-to-Speech (CosyVoice)**

import requests  
   
resp \= requests.post(  
    "https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/api/v1/services/aigc/text2audio/generation",  
	headers={  
    	"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.getenv('DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY')}",  
    	"Content-Type": "application/json"  
	},  
	json={  
    	"model": "cosyvoice-v3-plus",  
    	"input": {"text": "Hello, welcome to the Qwen Cloud Hackathon\!"},  
    	"parameters": {"voice": "default"}  
	}  
)  
 

   
**API Reference & Model Catalog**

 

■  **API Endpoints (Base URL: dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com)**

| Endpoint | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| POST /compatible-mode/v1/chat/completions | OpenAI-compatible chat completions (text & vision) |
| POST /compatible-mode/v1/responses | OpenAI Responses API (multi-step agent workflows) |
| POST /apps/anthropic/v1/messages | Anthropic-compatible messages endpoint |
| POST /api/v1/.../text-generation/generation | DashScope native text generation |
| POST /services/aigc/multimodal-generation/generation | Image generation (sync) |
| POST /services/aigc/text2image/image-synthesis | Image generation (async, poll with task ID) |
| GET /tasks/{task\_id} | Poll async task status |

■  **Available Models**

| Model ID | Type | Best For |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| qwen3.7-max | Text \+ Reasoning | Complex tasks |
| qwen3.7-plus | Text \+ Vision | Balanced perf. |
| qwen3.6-flash | Text | Fast & cheap |
| happyhorse-1.1-t2v | Text → Video | Video generation |
| happyhorse-1.1-i2v | Image → Video | Video from images |
| wan2.6-t2i | Text → Image | Image generation |
| qwen-image-2.0-pro | Image gen \+ edit | Creative assets |
| cosyvoice-v3-plus | Text → Speech | Voice / TTS |

*Free tier: 70M+ tokens available for all new accounts.* 

■  **Key Links for Participants**

| Resource | URL |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Hackathon Page** | qwencloud.com/challenge/hackathon |
| **Documentation** | docs.qwencloud.com |
| **API Key Portal** | home.qwencloud.com/api-keys |
| **Model Catalog** | qwencloud.com/models |
| **Interactive Playground** | home.qwencloud.com/try-ai |
| **GitHub** | github.com/QwenCloud |
| **Devpost (Submissions)** | devpost.com (search Qwen Cloud) |
| **Discord Community** | Linked from hackathon page |

   
**Sample Screenshot**  
Find your Workbench Overview and take a screenshot of the running project to show proof of Alibaba Cloud Deployment.  
**![][image1]**  
   
![][image2]

**Token Plan vs. Pay-as-you-go**

|   | Pay-as-you-go | Token Plan |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **API Key Prefix** | sk-xxxxx | sk-sp-xxxxx |
| **Base URL** | dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com /compatible-mode/v1 | token-plan.ap-southeast-1 .maas.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1 |
| **Billing** | Per-token, monthly invoice | Credit pool deducted per request |
| **Rate Limits** | Standard | Higher limits |
| **Usage Scope** | All use cases | Interactive dev tools (no backend scripts) |
| **Important:** Do NOT mix Token Plan keys with pay-as-you-go endpoints (or vice versa). Doing so returns 401 InvalidApiKey. |  |  |

 

■  **Token Plan Base URLs**

| Protocol | Base URL |
| :---: | :---: |
| **OpenAI Compatible** | https://token-plan.ap-southeast-1.maas.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1 |
| **Anthropic Compatible** | https://token-plan.ap-southeast-1.maas.aliyuncs.com/apps/anthropic |

 

■  **Token Plan — Python (OpenAI SDK)**

Install: pip install openai

import os  
from openai import OpenAI  
   
client \= OpenAI(  
    api\_key=os.getenv("DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY"),  \# sk-sp-xxxxx  
    base\_url="https://token-plan.ap-southeast-1.maas.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1"  
)  
   
completion \= client.chat.completions.create(  
	model="qwen3.7-plus",  
	messages=\[  
    	{"role": "user", "content": "Build me an AI agent that summarizes documents."}  
	\]  
)  
   
print(completion.choices\[0\].message.content)  
 

■  **Token Plan — Node.js (OpenAI SDK)**

Install: npm install openai

import OpenAI from "openai";  
   
const openai \= new OpenAI({  
	apiKey: process.env.DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY,  // sk-sp-xxxxx  
	baseURL: "https://token-plan.ap-southeast-1.maas.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1"  
});  
   
const completion \= await openai.chat.completions.create({  
	model: "qwen3.7-plus",  
	messages: \[  
    	{ role: "user", content: "Build me an AI agent that summarizes documents." }  
	\]  
});  
   
console.log(completion.choices\[0\].message.content);  
 

■  **Token Plan — cURL**

curl \-X POST https://token-plan.ap-southeast-1.maas.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1/chat/completions \\  
  \-H "Authorization: Bearer $DASHSCOPE\_API\_KEY" \\  
  \-H "Content-Type: application/json" \\  
  \-d '{  
	"model": "qwen3.7-plus",  
	"messages": \[  
  	{ "role": "user", "content": "Build me an AI agent." }  
	\]  
  }'  
 

**How To Deploy on Alibaba Cloud**

### Create the ECS Instance

#### Via the Console

1\.     Log in to the ECS Console and click **Create Instance**.

2\.     Select **Custom Launch** for full control.

3\.     Choose a **billing method**

4\.     Select **Region & Zone**.

5\.     Pick your **Instance Type** from the families listed above.

6\.     Choose an **Image** — Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 LTS is recommended for AI workloads.

7\.     Configure **Storage**

8\.     Set up **Networking**.

9\.     Attach a **Security Group**.

10\.  Set **Login Credentials** — use a Key Pair (recommended over passwords).

11\.  Under **Advanced**, set a descriptive instance name, optionally attach a RAM role, and add User Data scripts for automated bootstrapping.

12\.  Review, accept the Terms of Service, and click **Create Order**.

### Set Up Networking

**VPC:** Create a VPC with a private CIDR block (e.g., 172.16.0.0/12). Create VSwitches across multiple zones if you plan to use Auto Scaling or load balancing later.

**Public IP:** Either assign a public IPv4 address directly (pay-by-bandwidth or pay-by-traffic) or attach an Elastic IP (EIP) for flexible association. For backend instances that don't serve internet traffic directly, skip the public IP and use a NAT Gateway for outbound access.

**Security Groups:** These act as whitelists (deny-all by default). Open only the ports your agent needs:

| Port | Protocol | Source | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 22 | TCP | Your IP only | SSH access |
| 80 | TCP | 0.0.0.0/0 | HTTP (if serving web traffic) |
| 443 | TCP | 0.0.0.0/0 | HTTPS |
| 8080 | TCP | Your IP or LB | Agent API (if applicable) |

 

Never open SSH (22) or RDP to 0.0.0.0/0 in production.

### Connect to the Instance

| Method | Best for |
| :---- | :---- |
| Workbench (browser-based) | Quick access, no local software needed |
| SSH (OpenSSH / PuTTY) | Standard terminal access |
| VNC Console | Troubleshooting when SSH fails |
| Session Manager | Auditable access without a public IP |

 

\# SSH with key pair  
 ssh \-i \~/.ssh/my-alibaba-key.pem root@\<public-ip\>  
    
 \# Harden: disable password auth  
 sudo sed \-i 's/^PasswordAuthentication yes/PasswordAuthentication no/' /etc/ssh/sshd\_config  
 sudo systemctl restart sshd

### Install Dependencies

#### Docker (recommended)

\# Add Docker repo  
 curl \-fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg \--dearmor \-o /usr/share/keyrings/docker.gpg  
 echo "deb \[arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker.gpg\] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(lsb\_release \-cs) stable" | \\  
   sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list  
 sudo apt-get update  
 sudo apt-get install \-y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin  
    
 \# Start and enable  
 sudo systemctl start docker && sudo systemctl enable docker  
    
 \# Allow non-root Docker usage  
 sudo usermod \-aG docker $USER

#### Python (bare-metal)

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install \-y python3 python3-pip python3-venv git  
    
 \# Optional: Miniconda for AI workloads  
 wget https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86\_64.sh  
 bash Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86\_64.sh

#### Node.js

curl \-fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup\_20.x | sudo \-E bash \-  
 sudo apt-get install \-y nodejs

### Deploy the Agent

#### Option A: Docker Compose (multi-service)

This is the recommended approach for agents that need supporting services (database, cache, reverse proxy).

\# docker-compose.yml  
 version: '3.8'  
 services:  
   agent:  
 	build: .  
 	ports:  
   	\- "8080:8080"  
 	environment:  
   	\- API\_KEY=${API\_KEY}  
   	\- LOG\_LEVEL=info  
 	volumes:  
   	\- ./data:/app/data  
 	restart: unless-stopped  
 	deploy:  
   	resources:  
     	reservations:  
       	devices:  
         	\- driver: nvidia  
           	count: all  
           	capabilities: \[gpu\]  
    
   redis:  
 	image: redis:7-alpine  
 	ports:  
   	\- "6379:6379"  
 	restart: unless-stopped  
    
   nginx:  
 	image: nginx:alpine  
 	ports:  
   	\- "80:80"  
   	\- "443:443"  
 	volumes:  
   	\- ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf  
   	\- ./certs:/etc/nginx/certs  
 	restart: unless-stopped  
 	depends\_on:  
   	\- agent

docker compose up \-d

#### Option B: Single Container

docker run \-d \\  
   \--name ai-agent \\  
   \--restart unless-stopped \\  
   \-p 8080:8080 \\  
   \--env-file .env \\  
   \-v /opt/agent/data:/app/data \\  
   my-ai-agent:latest  
    
 \# With GPU support:  
 docker run \-d \--gpus all \--name ai-agent \-p 8080:8080 my-ai-agent:latest

#### Option C: Bare-Metal with systemd

\# Clone and set up  
 git clone https://github.com/your-org/ai-agent.git /opt/ai-agent  
 cd /opt/ai-agent  
 python3 \-m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate  
 pip install \-r requirements.txt

Create a systemd service for persistence:

\# /etc/systemd/system/ai-agent.service  
 \[Unit\]  
 Description=AI Agent Application  
 After=network.target  
    
 \[Service\]  
 Type=simple  
 User=agent  
 WorkingDirectory=/opt/ai-agent  
 ExecStart=/opt/ai-agent/venv/bin/python main.py  
 Restart=always  
 RestartSec=10  
 Environment=NODE\_ENV=production  
 StandardOutput=journal  
 StandardError=journal  
    
 \[Install\]  
 WantedBy=multi-user.target

sudo systemctl daemon-reload  
 sudo systemctl enable ai-agent  
 sudo systemctl start ai-agent  
 sudo systemctl status ai-agent

## 

## Deploying an Agent on Alibaba Cloud SAS

### When to Use SAS Instead of ECS

Simple Application Server (SAS/SWAS) bundles compute, storage, and networking into predictable monthly plans. It trades flexibility for simplicity.

 

| Choose SAS if... | Choose ECS instead if... |
| :---- | :---- |
| You need a single agent server for dev/test or low-traffic production | You need GPU instances for local model inference |
| You want fixed, predictable pricing with no surprise bills | You need Auto Scaling or load balancing |
| You want to deploy in under 5 minutes | You need full VPC control or VPC peering |
| Your agent calls external LLM APIs (no local GPU needed) | Your workload is high-concurrency or mission-critical |
| You're an individual developer, student, or small team | You need multiple data disks or specialized storage |

**Best-fit agent types for SAS:** LLM API wrapper agents, Dify / MaxKB deployments, lightweight RAG pipelines, chatbot backends, webhook-based automation agents.

### Create the Instance

1\.     Go to the [SAS Console](https://www.alibabacloud.com/en/product/swas?_p_lc=1&spm=a2c65.11461447.0.0.155b14e0JKYSdI).

2\.     Click **Create Server**.

3\.     Select a **Region**.

4\.     Select an **Image**.

5\.     Select your **Plan**

6\.     Choose **quantity** and **subscription duration**.

7\.     Complete payment. The instance is provisioned immediately with an auto-assigned public IP.

**Important:** SAS instances have no default password. Before connecting, reset the root password from the console under **Reset Password**.

 

### Choose an Image

#### Application Images (pre-installed software — fastest path)

These come with a ready-to-use software stack on top of Alibaba Cloud Linux 3:

| Image | Best for |
| :---- | :---- |
| Dify v0.15.3 | LLM app platform deployment |
| MaxKB v1.10.2 | Knowledge-base chatbot |
| Docker v26.1.3 | Custom containerized agent |
| LAMP / LNMP | Web-based agent dashboards |
| BT-Panel / 1Panel | Non-technical server management |

 

**For a custom agent, the Docker image is the recommended starting point.**

### Connect to the Instance

#### Linux

 

| Method | How |
| :---- | :---- |
| Workbench (recommended) | Click "Connect" on the instance card in the console. Logs in as root via internal auth — no password needed. |
| SSH | Use any SSH client. Connect as \`root\` with the password you set in Step 3\. |
| Rescue Mode (VNC) | Use when SSH fails (e.g., instance under heavy load). Console \> Connect \> Rescue. Auto-disconnects after \~5 min inactivity. |

 

\# SSH from your terminal  
 ssh root@\<public-ip\>

#### Windows

Use **Workbench** (RDP via browser) or **Remote Desktop** (mstsc) with the Administrator password you set in the console.

 

### Configure the Firewall

SAS has a built-in firewall that controls inbound traffic only (outbound is unrestricted). It supports up to 50 rules per instance.

**Default rules (Linux):** TCP 22, 80, 443 \+ ICMP.

Open the ports your agent needs:

| Port | Protocol | Source | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 22 | TCP | Your IP range only | SSH (restrict source) |
| 80 | TCP | 0.0.0.0/0 | HTTP |
| 443 | TCP | 0.0.0.0/0 | HTTPS |
| 8080 | TCP | 0.0.0.0/0 or restricted | Agent API endpoint |

To add a rule: SAS Console \> your instance \> **Firewall** tab \> **Add Rule** \> select a preset (HTTP, SSH, MySQL, etc.) or define a custom port range.

**Security tip:** Restrict SSH (port 22\) to your known IP ranges rather than leaving it open to all sources.

 

### Install Dependencies

#### Option A: Docker Application Image (recommended)

If you selected the Docker image at creation, Docker Engine and Docker Compose are already installed. Verify:

docker \--version    	\# Docker version 26.1.3  
 docker compose version  \# Docker Compose version 2.x

Skip ahead to Section 8\.

### Option B: Manual Install on a Clean OS Image

**Docker (Ubuntu):**

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade \-y  
 sudo apt-get install \-y ca-certificates curl gnupg  
    
 \# Add Docker's official GPG key and repo  
 sudo install \-m 0755 \-d /etc/apt/keyrings  
 curl \-fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | \\  
   sudo gpg \--dearmor \-o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg  
 echo "deb \[arch=$(dpkg \--print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg\] \\  
   https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(lsb\_release \-cs) stable" | \\  
   sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list \> /dev/null  
    
 sudo apt-get update  
 sudo apt-get install \-y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin  
 sudo systemctl start docker && sudo systemctl enable docker  
 sudo usermod \-aG docker $USER

**Python (bare-metal):**

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install \-y python3 python3-pip python3-venv git curl

**Node.js:**

curl \-fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup\_20.x | sudo \-E bash \-  
 sudo apt-get install \-y nodejs

 

### Deploy the Agent

#### Option A: Docker Compose (recommended)

Best for agents with supporting services (database, cache, reverse proxy).

\# docker-compose.yml  
 version: '3.8'  
 services:  
   agent:  
 	build: .  
 	ports:  
   	\- "8080:8080"  
 	environment:  
   	\- API\_KEY=${API\_KEY}  
   	\- LOG\_LEVEL=info  
 	volumes:  
   	\- ./data:/app/data  
 	restart: unless-stopped  
 	mem\_limit: 3g    	\# Leave headroom for the OS  
    
   redis:  
 	image: redis:7-alpine  
 	ports:  
   	\- "6379:6379"  
 	restart: unless-stopped  
    
   nginx:  
 	image: nginx:alpine  
 	ports:  
   	\- "80:80"  
   	\- "443:443"  
 	volumes:  
   	\- ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf  
   	\- ./certs:/etc/nginx/certs  
 	restart: unless-stopped  
 	depends\_on:  
   	\- agent

\# Create a .env file with your secrets  
 echo "API\_KEY=your-api-key-here" \> .env  
    
 \# Start the stack  
 docker compose up \-d  
    
 \# Verify  
 docker compose ps  
 docker compose logs \-f agent

#### Option B: Single Container

docker run \-d \\  
   \--name ai-agent \\  
   \--restart unless-stopped \\  
   \--memory 3g \\  
   \-p 8080:8080 \\  
   \--env-file .env \\  
   \-v /opt/agent/data:/app/data \\  
   my-ai-agent:latest

#### Option C: Bare-Metal with Process Manager

\# Clone and set up  
 git clone https://github.com/your-org/ai-agent.git /opt/ai-agent  
 cd /opt/ai-agent  
 python3 \-m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate  
 pip install \-r requirements.txt  
    
 \# Option 1: systemd service  
 sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/ai-agent.service \<\< 'EOF'  
 \[Unit\]  
 Description=AI Agent Application  
 After=network.target  
    
 \[Service\]  
 Type=simple  
 User=agent  
 WorkingDirectory=/opt/ai-agent  
 ExecStart=/opt/ai-agent/venv/bin/python main.py  
 Restart=always  
 RestartSec=10  
 StandardOutput=journal  
 StandardError=journal  
    
 \[Install\]  
 WantedBy=multi-user.target  
 EOF  
    
 sudo systemctl daemon-reload  
 sudo systemctl enable ai-agent && sudo systemctl start ai-agent  
    
 \# Option 2: PM2 (Node.js agents)  
 npm install \-g pm2  
 pm2 start main.js \--name ai-agent  
 pm2 save && pm2 startup

 

#### Deploying Dify (Quick Start with Application Image)

If you chose the **Dify** application image at creation, the platform is already installed with Ollama and sample models. Here's how to get it running:

* Connect via Workbench or SSH.  
* Check the Dify service status: docker compose ps (in the Dify project directory).  
* Open port 80 (or the configured port) in the firewall.  
*  Navigate to http://\<public-ip\> in your browser.  
* Create an admin account on first visit.  
* Configure your LLM API keys (Qwen Cloud, etc.) in Settings \> Model Provider.

 

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image2]: <data:image/png;base64,>
